cryptoTax 0.1.0.30

  • News

Add pricing objects to the cryptoTax cache

Source: R/pricing_cache_helpers.R
add_to_cache.Rd

Seed the package-owned pricing cache explicitly from objects already available in the current R session, for example after loading a previously saved list.prices or USD2CAD.table from disk.

This is especially useful for multi-day tax workflows where you want to keep explicit saved pricing files on disk, but still let formatter helpers reuse those objects through the package cache during the current R session.

All supplied objects are validated before being cached. Invalid objects fail early with a clear error instead of silently poisoning later pricing or FX resolution.

Usage

add_to_cache(list.prices = NULL, coins.list = NULL, USD2CAD.table = NULL)

Arguments

list.prices

Optional list.prices object to store in the package cache.

coins.list

Optional output from crypto2::crypto_list() to store in the package cache.

USD2CAD.table

Optional USD/CAD rate table to store in the package cache.

Value

Invisibly returns TRUE.

Examples

add_to_cache(list.prices = list_prices_example)
pricing_cache("list.prices")
#>       timestamp        slug        name currency        open       close
#> 1    2021-01-01     bitcoin     Bitcoin      BTC 45000.00000 45000.00000
#> 2    2021-01-02     bitcoin     Bitcoin      BTC 45035.00000 45035.00000
